content: The word "lisp" has its roots in the Old English word "wlisp" or "wliþsp", and the modern English spelling with an "s" was likely influenced by the Middle English pronunciation. The Oxford English Dictionary attributes the spelling to the influence of the Old Norse word "vispa", which also contained the "s" sound. Over time, the spelling of the word "lisp" became standardized with the "s".
